task: Root-cause analysis of GBP_JPY #4507 premature stop-loss hit
local_output: Trade lost due to SL being too tight
opus_correction: The Dynamic SL in trending/continuing branch computed E55+8p=210.774 and moved SL from 211.097 to 210.774 — 32.3 pips tighter in ONE tick. Root cause: no minimum distance from entry (retracing branch had 50% floor but trending/continuing had none), and no max-tighten-per-tick limit (Phase 3 retrace trail uses 15-30% per tick but main Dynamic SL has no rate limit). Fix: universal 50% original SL floor across all branches + 30% per-tick rate limit when gap exceeds 5p.
opus_reasoning: Traced code path through position_guardian.py line 2990. Identified that the continuing branch gate conditions all passed (candles>=5, peak>=3.0, pnl>0). Buffer computed as max(8.0, min(15.0, atr*0.8))=8.0p floor. New SL=E55+buffer jumped from 41.8p to 9.5p from entry in one tick. Compared to retracing branch which already had the 50% safeguard. Verified fix via numerical replay: with both fixes, SL after tick 1 would be 211.007 (32.8p room) instead of 210.774 (9.5p room). Retrace to ~210.77 would not have triggered the stop.

task: Daily scout performance analysis with gate attribution
opus_correction: 5 trades, 3W/2L (60% WR), +$83.16. Key insight: scout-to-trade attribution is completely broken — all 5 trades have finding_id=NULL despite 10 matching scout findings on the same pairs. Oscillator gate blocked 73 entries into exhausted GBP_USD SELL positions (RSI 25-28, Stoch 1-5), serving as the most impactful filter. Fewer trades (5 vs 16 yesterday) with higher quality = more profit (+$83 vs -$182). Action items: fix finding_id attribution pipeline, add BB width thresholds, deprioritize low-WR strategies S16 (44%) and V4_EARLY_WARNING (47%).
opus_reasoning: Queried trading_forex.db for all trades on 2026-04-03. Cross-referenced scout findings table to verify attribution gap. Analyzed gate rejection logs to identify oscillator gate as primary blocker (73 rejections). Compared day-over-day metrics: yesterday 16 trades/-$182/69% WR vs today 5 trades/+$83/60% WR — demonstrating that gate throttling is working correctly by reducing overtrading. Computed 30-day lookback strategy statistics to identify underperformers.
